- Node 111: rows 57-59, cols 8-9 - This is at col 8-9, which is to the left
So Node 111 is on the left (col 8-9) and Node 110 is on the right (col 20-22). But the blue panel is at [[54, 17], [54, 25], [62, 25], [62, 17]], which is at cols 17-25. So the blue panel is on the right, and the gray panel is on the left.
Wait, but Node 111 is at cols 8-9, which is to the left of the blue panel (cols 17-25). So Node 111 is in the gray panel (left), and Node 110 is in the blue panel (right).
Actually, let me re-examine. The blue panel is at [[54, 17], [54, 25], [62, 25], [62, 17]], which means it spans rows 54-62 and cols 17-25. Node 110 is at [[55, 20], [55, 22], [56, 22], [56, 20]], which is within the blue panel's area (rows 55-56, cols 20-22). So Node 110 is in the blue panel.
Node 111 is at [[57, 8], [57, 9], [59, 9], [59, 8]], which is at cols 8-9, to the left of the blue panel. So Node 111 is in the gray panel.
So the current state is:
- Gray panel (left): contains Node 111 (yellow object)
- Blue panel (right): contains Node 110 (yellow object)
Previously (initial state):
- Blue panel (left): contained a yellow object
- Gray panel (right): contained a yellow object
So the panels have swapped positions! This confirms that clicking on the yellow object in the gray panel (which was on the right initially) caused the panels to swap.
Now, the left panel's object has changed from a square to a U-shape. This matches the top yellow U-shaped object in the right panel.
I think the goal is to match the left panel's object shape to the bottom yellow square object in the right panel. To do this, I need to click on the yellow object in the blue panel (which is now on the right) to swap the panels back, and then click on something else to change the left panel's object shape.
Actually, wait. Let me think about this more carefully. The left panel's object changed from a square to a U-shape when I clicked on the yellow object in the gray panel (which was on the right). This suggests that clicking on the yellow object in the gray panel selects the U-shape mode, and clicking on the yellow object in the blue panel selects the square mode.
But the panels have swapped positions. So now, the gray panel is on the left and the blue panel is on the right. If I click on the yellow object in the blue panel (Node 110, at rows 55-56, cols 20-22), it should swap the panels back and change the left panel's object shape.
